1.notes: Opening par 4 at TPC Southwind. Straightaway hole with water on the left side. Requires accurate tee shot to set up approach.

2.notes: Short par 4 with water in play. Risk/reward hole where aggressive players can attack the green.

3.notes: Par 5 with water down the entire left side. Three-shot hole for most players. Green is protected by bunkers.

4.notes: Short par 3 over water. Island-style green demands precision. Bunkers front and back.

hazards.notes: Water surrounds most of the green

5.notes: Long par 4 with dogleg left. Water guards the left side. Bunkers pinch the landing area.

6.notes: Long par 4 with slight dogleg right. Water on right side threatens approach shots.

7.notes: Long par 4 with dogleg right. Strategic bunkering throughout. Green slopes severely.

8.notes: Short par 3 with water front and right. Precision required to hit the putting surface.

9.notes: Signature hole. Long dogleg right with lake in front of green. Second shot over water. Large tree guards the corner.

10.notes: Long par 4 to start the back nine. Water left, bunkers right. Demands accuracy.

11.notes: Famous island green par 3, mini version of TPC Sawgrass 17th. Short iron over water to small island green with pot bunker.

hazards.notes: Water completely surrounds the island green
Small pot bunker at front edge

12.notes: Mid-length par 4 with bunkers guarding both sides of the fairway and green.

13.notes: Long par 4 with dogleg left. Water threatens the aggressive line. Green has multiple tiers.

14.notes: One of the toughest par 3s on Tour. Elevated tee, full carry over water to severely undulating green. Natural amphitheater setting.

15.notes: Shorter par 4 offering birdie chance. Precision more important than power.

16.notes: Reachable par 5 for long hitters. Water guards the right side. Risk/reward second shot.

17.notes: Long demanding par 4. Bunkers and water challenge both shots. One of the hardest holes.

18.notes: Excellent finishing hole. Dogleg left with water along entire left side. Fairway bunkers right. Spectator mounds surround green.

1.notes: Opening par 4 at Quail Hollow. Downhill tee shot to wide fairway. Green protected by bunkers.

2.notes: Dogleg left par 4 playing uphill. Creek crosses in front of the green.

3.notes: Short par 3 with water front right. Bunkers surround the green.

4.notes: Par 5 with dogleg right. Reachable in two for long hitters. Creek guards green.

5.notes: Long par 4 playing uphill. Narrow landing area with bunkers on both sides.

6.notes: Mid-length par 4 with slight downhill slope. Creek crosses in front of green.

7.notes: Par 5 dogleg left. Water guards the left side. Strategic bunkering throughout.

8.notes: Short par 4. Risk/reward hole where bombers can get close to the green.

9.notes: Long par 4 to finish the front. Uphill approach to elevated green.

10.notes: Par 5 with sharp dogleg right. Water right side. Reachable but risky.

11.notes: Par 3 playing slightly uphill. Deep bunkers protect the green.

12.notes: Long par 4 with dogleg left. Water left threatens aggressive play.

13.notes: Mid-length par 4. Requires precision off the tee to set up approach.

14.notes: Par 4 with dogleg right. Water guards right side. Challenging approach.

15.notes: Par 3 playing uphill. Long iron or hybrid required. Bunkers front and back.

16.notes: Start of the Green Mile. Demanding par 4 with water left and bunkers right. One of the hardest holes on Tour.

17.notes: Green Mile hole 2. Dramatic downhill par 3 to peninsula green. Water surrounds three sides.

18.notes: Green Mile finale. Demanding uphill par 4 with water left. Dramatic finishing hole with spectator amphitheater.

1.notes: The Post - Opening par 4. Classic Nicklaus design with strategic bunkering. Wide fairway to start.

2.notes: Winning Colors - Long par 4 with water left. Named after Kentucky Derby winner. Demands precision.

3.notes: Honest Abe - Downhill par 3 with water front right. Named for Abraham Lincoln's Kentucky birthplace.

4.notes: Mine That Bird - Short par 4 with dogleg right. Risk/reward hole. Named after 2009 Derby winner.

5.notes: The Sun Shines Bright - Long par 4 playing uphill. Named after Kentucky state song. Demanding tee shot.

6.notes: Long Shot - Long par 4 with dogleg left. Water left side. One of the harder holes.

7.notes: Genuine Risk - Reachable par 5 with dogleg right. Water right guards aggressive play. Named after 1980 Kentucky Derby winner.

8.notes: Float Like A Butterfly - Uphill par 3 with water front. Named after Louisville's Muhammad Ali.

9.notes: Twin Spires - Par 4 to finish front nine. Named after Churchill Downs. Bunkers guard the green.

10.notes: Big Red - Par 5 named after Secretariat. Reachable in two but water left punishes misses.

11.notes: Holler - Par 3 with water front and left. Downhill shot to green with severe slopes.

12.notes: Sting Like A Bee - Long par 4 with dogleg right. Another Ali tribute. Water right guards approach.

13.notes: The Limestone Hole - Short par 4 with limestone rock features. Risk/reward driveable hole.

14.notes: On The Rocks - Long par 3 with water front and right. Demanding iron shot to well-guarded green.

15.notes: Julep - Par 4 playing uphill. Named after Kentucky's signature drink. Precision required.

16.notes: Homestretch - Long par 4. Named after the stretch run at Churchill Downs. Water left threatens approach.

17.notes: Straight Up - Par 4 with slight dogleg left. Downhill tee shot. Sets up dramatic finish.

18.notes: Photo Finish - Dramatic finishing par 5 with water left and right. Site of Tiger Woods vs Bob May playoff in 2000 PGA Championship.
